title: Make mkproj init tolerate pre-rendered instill manifest
description:
## Why this issue exists
mkproj Phase 1 writes .claude/skill-manifest.json before Phase 2 shells out to instill init. A real fresh-project repro shows instill init exits with 'manifest already exists; use --force to reinitialize', so full live mkproj init verification is blocked.

## What needs to be done
Adjust the mkproj init lifecycle so the instill step succeeds when the scaffold writer has already rendered the skill manifest, and add regression coverage that proves the real command contract.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] a failing automated test captures the current init-step argument mismatch when a manifest already exists
- [ ] mkproj uses the correct instill init invocation for the pre-rendered manifest flow
- [ ] init tests prove the expected instill init args and no existing verified hook-chain behavior regresses
- [ ] GOCACHE=$PWD/.cache/go-build go test ./internal/init -count=1 passes
- [ ] GOCACHE=$PWD/.cache/go-build go test ./... -count=1 passes

title: Validate and address mkproj review findings from docs/initial_implementation
description: Why: docs/initial_implementation/reviews/mkproj-review-handoff-2026-06-22.md lists 14 critical/important findings from an external review, but they have not all been independently verified against the current implementation state at ca50c7b. What to build: verify each finding against repo reality and SPEC/ADR intent, classify each as fully correct / partially correct / not correct, and implement only the fixes supported by that verification. Record any findings that map to already-open beads rather than silently re-scoping them. Acceptance: every review finding is evaluated against code/spec; valid defects are fixed with tests first; invalid or partial findings are documented with concrete evidence; full verification passes before close.
notes: Verified docs/initial_implementation/reviews/mkproj-review-handoff-2026-06-22.md against current HEAD ca50c7b. Disposition: 1 embed.FS absent = NOT CORRECT (assets.go embeds templates/sources); 2 walking-skeleton test absent = PARTIALLY CORRECT concern, NOT literal claim (cmd/mkproj/walking_skeleton_test.go exists, but broader smoke scope remains tracked in uuw/wqh.1); 3 gate-pipeline files missing = NOT CORRECT (all 6 stacks carry .mkproj-overlay/{mise.toml,lefthook.yml,.github/workflows/ci.yml} and gate_assets_test covers them); 4 sync-allowlist data-loss via 5-entry default block = NOT CORRECT (allowlist.CanonicalBlock renders canonical managed block from template); 5 template secret-scan broken = NOT CORRECT (scan_staged present; writer test enforces template/shared parity); 6 python templates use ProjectName = NOT CORRECT (pyproject templates use PythonPackage); 7 csharp templates hardcode Project = NOT CORRECT (csproj templates use CSharpNamespace); 8 skill manifest includes go skills for all languages = NOT CORRECT (template gates Go skills by .Language); 9 CONTEXT stub + ADR scaffold missing = NOT CORRECT (templates/common includes both; writer test asserts render); 10 sources.yaml placeholder SHAs = NOT CORRECT (real refs/resolved versions present); 11 github-user prompt missing for --remote gh = NOT CORRECT (prompt.Resolve prompts and tests cover it); 12 allowlist.Detect swallows Sscanf errors = NOT CORRECT (wrapped parse error returned); 13 mustGetwd panic = NOT CORRECT (currentWorkingDir returns error; no panic helper exists); 14 ensureEmptyDir dead code = NOT CORRECT (ensureWritableDir is the live helper and Writer.Write calls it). Fresh verification: GOCACHE=/Users/poconnor/peter_code/exploratory_repos/agentic_template_start/.worktrees/codex-mkproj-template-system/.cache/go-build go test ./... -count=1 passed. bats test/secret-scan.bats could not be run in this environment because bats is not installed on PATH.
close_reason: External review handoff was verified against current HEAD ca50c7b. No new current-code defects from that document remained to fix; the only partially valid concern is broader walking-skeleton/smoke coverage, which is already tracked by agentic_template_start-uuw and agentic_template_start-wqh.1.

title: Address mkproj compliance review blockers
description: Why: The 2026-06-22 compliance review found shipping blockers and important contract mismatches in the mkproj implementation worktree. What to build: verify and fix the confirmed blockers before any further user testing, including embed.FS packaging, template/security defects, template variable correctness, allowlist sync correctness, and any user-facing prompt/contract mismatches proven by the review. Acceptance: confirmed review blockers are fixed with tests, remaining non-fixed findings are explicitly documented as still-open follow-up work, and full repo verification passes.

title: FEATURE: Enforced gate pipeline resolves across all stacks
description: Integration Feature owning the CI-resolves seam (SPEC.md §18): the language-agnostic ci.yml (ud1) must invoke a 'ci' mise task that the scaffolded mise.toml (x2k) actually defines, for every shipped v1 stack. This Feature's gate is the cross-stack resolve test — neither child alone owns it.
acceptance_criteria: SEAM TEST (gate): for every shipped v1 stack, the scaffolded .github/workflows/ci.yml runs 'mise install' then 'mise run ci' with no inline lint/test/format, AND 'mise run ci' resolves to a 'ci' task defined in that stack's mise.toml. All child stories (ud1, x2k) closed.
close_reason: Verified on 2026-06-23: shipped v1 overlays carry scaffolded mise.toml/lefthook.yml/ci.yml assets, ci.yml delegates only to 'mise install' then 'mise run ci', and focused verification passed with GOCACHE=/Users/poconnor/peter_code/exploratory_repos/agentic_template_start/.cache/go-build go test ./internal/scaffold ./cmd/mkproj ./test -count=1.

title: FEATURE: Walking-skeleton scaffold composes and runs offline
description: Integration Feature owning the composition seam (SPEC.md §18): the Phase-1 writer (iha) composes vanilla + overlay from the catalog assets (3tt) and common assets (yuw) per the CLI/render contract (83n) into a walking skeleton. This Feature's gate is the seam test — no single child story owns the end-to-end composition proof. Pulled forward as the FIRST user-visible value milestone of the epic.
acceptance_criteria: SEAM TEST (gate): 'mkproj init --stack <key> --remote none' composes the tree correctly (vanilla-first, overlay-wins, CLAUDE.md symlink, deterministic .gitignore merge), then 'mise install && mise run ci' exits 0 running >=1 real passing test, fully offline (assert no network). MAY be proven with one stack first; MUST cover all six v1 stacks to close. All child stories (83n, iha, 3tt, yuw) closed.
close_reason: Six-stack offline seam coverage is in place; real go-cli init --remote none + mise install + mise run ci verified; remote-tier work continues in agentic_template_start-uuw

title: mkproj v1 — one command scaffolds a complete AI-native project
description: Umbrella epic for the mkproj v1 system. One command turns an empty directory into a fully configured, AI-native project (git, agent config, beads, skills, security floor, quality gates, optional remote) — indistinguishable from hand-built, zero follow-up. Canonical docs: docs/PRD.md (intent), docs/SPEC.md (truth), docs/adr/0001-0009 (decisions), CONTEXT.md (glossary). v1 catalog = Go, Python, C# (six stacks). All non-seam stories sit flat under this epic; three Features wrap the integration seams (see SPEC.md §18).
acceptance_criteria: The canonical product acceptance test passes: 'mkproj init' in an empty folder yields a complete working project with >=1 real passing test, active security floor, and gates running locally and in CI, with no manual steps after. All child Features and flat stories are closed.

title: Add Audit/Security + Coverage sections to golang/python/csharp guideline files
description: The mkproj overlay (v1) installs StyleCop.Analyzers (C#), govulncheck (Go), pip-audit (Python), dotnet list package --vulnerable (C#), coverage tools (go test -cover / pytest-cov / coverlet), pyright (Python), and dotnet format (C#). ADR-0003 makes the guideline files canonical: the ebp conformance test fails if the overlay installs a tool the guideline does not mandate. These tools are not yet written into the guideline files. Add an 'Audit/Security + Coverage' section to each so every overlay tool traces to a written MUST/SHOULD. NUnit->xUnit contradiction already fixed in csharp.md on 2026-06-19. Source: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-19-mkproj-cli-prompt-render-contract.md §7.
acceptance_criteria: golang.md names govulncheck + coverage; python.md names pip-audit + pytest-cov + pyright; csharp.md names StyleCop.Analyzers + dotnet list package --vulnerable + coverlet + dotnet format; every tool in the overlay table traces to a MUST/SHOULD line

title: Add guideline-to-template conformance tests for shipped overlays
description:
## Why this exists
The language guideline files are the canonical source of truth, but no issue currently makes that contract executable.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] automated tests cover the shipped v1 languages and fail if an overlay drops a mandated tool or task
- [ ] tests fail if a shipped template exists without a matching guideline-backed language
- [ ] tests are runnable from the Go repo with a documented `go test` target or package
acceptance_criteria: automated tests cover the shipped v1 languages and FAIL when a guideline MUST/SHOULD has no corresponding overlay tool (a missing mandated tool); tests do NOT fail when the overlay ships a vetted extra the guideline is silent on (floor semantics, ADR-0010); tests fail if a shipped template exists for a language with no guideline file; tests are runnable from the Go repo with a documented 'go test' target or package; the three guideline files are resolvable at their canonical path (depends on zz8 publishing them).
notes: Floor-not-ceiling conformance per ADR-0010 / SPEC §10.2: the check is one-directional (missing-MUST fails; extra-tool passes). FluentAssertions and pytest-mock are allowed vetted extras and must NOT trip the test. Blocked-by zz8: the guideline files must be published at their canonical path first.

title: Generate CI workflow templates that reuse mise run ci
description:
## Why this exists
The spec requires one gate definition with multiple callers. CI must consume `mise run ci` instead of duplicating local gate commands.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] workflow templates exist for the shipped v1 overlays that need CI
- [ ] workflows install required bootstrap tooling and invoke `mise run ci`
- [ ] lint/test commands are not duplicated directly in workflow YAML
- [ ] verification proves the referenced `mise` task names exist in the shipped overlays
notes: CI shape decided 2026-06-19 (grill-with-docs, Q13): (13a) ONE shared, language-agnostic ci.yml identical across all stacks — checkout -> install mise -> 'mise install' -> 'mise run ci'; no inline lint/test/format (mise handles toolchain variance). (13b) triggers: on [push, pull_request]; NO scheduled run for v1. (13c) verification asserts every shipped overlay's mise.toml defines a 'ci' task that 'mise run ci' resolves to — this is the contract coupling ud1 to x2k.
close_reason: Verified on 2026-06-23: shipped v1 overlays carry scaffolded mise.toml/lefthook.yml/ci.yml assets, ci.yml delegates only to 'mise install' then 'mise run ci', and focused verification passed with GOCACHE=/Users/poconnor/peter_code/exploratory_repos/agentic_template_start/.cache/go-build go test ./internal/scaffold ./cmd/mkproj ./test -count=1.

title: Implement mkproj Phase 3 remote publish flow
description:
## Why this exists
Remote creation is part of the standard happy path, but it MUST happen only after hooks and gates are wired so the first push goes through the full pipeline.

## What to build
Own `--remote gh`, `--remote url`, and `--remote none` behavior for mkproj init.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] interactive default is `--remote gh`
- [ ] `gh` and explicit URL flows run only after local gates and hook wiring are ready
- [ ] `gh` uses repo creation plus initial push; `url` uses `git remote add origin <url>` plus initial push; `none` performs local initial commit only
- [ ] failures identify whether repo creation, remote wiring, commit, or push failed
- [ ] the first commit/push invariant from the spec is preserved
notes: Phase 3 remote-failure decided 2026-06-19 (grill-with-docs, 10c): option A — if gh repo create succeeds but the push fails, LEAVE the orphaned remote and instruct the user (print URL plus 'git push -u origin <branch>' retry; mention gh repo delete as their option). NEVER auto-delete the remote (irreversible outward action; common cause is a gate tripping = working as designed). If gh repo create itself fails, report and remain local-only. Phase-3-last ordering guarantees a push failure is always recoverable.

title: Implement deny-only guard hook composition + dual-agent PreToolUse wiring
description:
## Why this exists
The secret-scan core is only one slice of the deny floor. The full guard hook and its Claude/Codex wiring are still missing.

## What to build
Create `.claude/hooks/guard` as the full deny-only guard, compose `.claude/hooks/secret-scan.sh scan-command`, and wire the same deny contract for Claude and Codex.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] the guard is project-relative and self-contained
- [ ] D1-D8 and D11-D16 block with clear stderr reasons
- [ ] D9/D10 are delegated to `secret-scan.sh scan-command`
- [ ] compound handling remains deny-only; there is no hook-side allow logic
- [ ] Claude and Codex PreToolUse wiring is verified with at least one blocked and one allowed command
- [ ] the issue text and implementation notes explicitly follow ADR-0002 and the allowlist seed corrections

title: Implement mkproj init entrypoint and Phase 2 delegate shell-outs
description:
## Why this exists
The current board has the pieces for secret-scan, hook verification, and scaffold assets, but no issue owns the actual mkproj CLI entrypoint and the ordered Phase 2 shell-out flow.

## What to build
Implement the Go CLI entrypoint for `mkproj init` and wire the ordered Phase 2 subprocess flow: repo bootstrap, guard-before-`bd init`, `bd init`, `instill init`, `pick-skills`, `check-skills`, and `lefthook install` at the correct point.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] the CLI entrypoint follows the approved contract from the CLI/prompt/render issue
- [ ] guard-related files/settings exist before `bd init`
- [ ] `bd init` happens before `lefthook install` and before any work that depends on beads hooks being present
- [ ] `instill init`, `pick-skills`, and `check-skills` run in the approved order
- [ ] failures stop the run with a step-specific error instead of continuing into later phases
- [ ] runtime hook installation uses the chain-mode behavior proven by `agentic_template_start-485`
notes: Init lifecycle decided 2026-06-19 (grill-with-docs): (10a) STRICT empty-dir precondition — refuse non-empty dir (allow ignorable cruft like .DS_Store), error 'directory not empty'. (10b) Failure posture C — fail-fast at the failed step, leave partial state, print the step name plus a recursive-force-delete recovery command for the dir; NO transactional rollback (safe because the dir is empty-by-precondition). Full BDD scenarios to be written into a forthcoming init-lifecycle spec.

title: Build mkproj Phase 1 scaffold writer from embed.FS
description:
## Why this exists
Phase 1 is mkproj-owned and MUST perform render/copy/link work before any delegated tools run.

## What to build
Implement the Phase 1 writer that reads embedded assets and applies the approved file-role contract to create the target repo contents.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Phase 1 ordering matches the design
- [ ] render vs verbatim copy vs link behavior is implemented and tested
- [ ] `.gitignore` merge behavior is deterministic
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` is created as a symlink to `AGENTS.md`
- [ ] a generated repo tree matches the expected Phase 1 output structure from the spec before `bd`/`instill` delegation begins
notes: Composition rule (grill-with-docs 2026-06-20, ADR-0005, from 3tt): a stack's golden tree = vanilla layer + .mkproj-overlay/. Phase 1 composes by copying VANILLA first, then overlaying .mkproj-overlay/ files ON TOP (last-writer-wins, overlay wins on path collision). The composed result must be a walking skeleton: runs end-to-end with >=1 green test. .mkproj-overlay/ is a packaging dir in the source repo, NOT a literal output dir — its contents are merged into the repo root at the corresponding paths, the .mkproj-overlay/ prefix is stripped.

title: Vendor shipped v1 catalog assets (golden snapshots + .mkproj-overlay packaging)
description:
## Why this exists
`mkproj init` is required to stay offline and unpack vendored assets rather than run native scaffolders live. The shipped v1 stacks therefore need committed golden snapshots and packaged overlays.

## What to package
Vendor the shipped v1 catalog assets under `templates/golden/`, including the pinned snapshot content and `.mkproj-overlay/` directories for each shipped stack.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] each shipped v1 stack has committed catalog assets under the expected stack key
- [ ] snapshots are pinned and usable offline at init time
- [ ] `.mkproj-overlay/` placement is explicit for every shipped stack
- [ ] this issue owns where the assets live and how they are packaged, while `agentic_template_start-x2k` continues to own the scaffolded `mise.toml` content itself
design: Design closed in grill-with-docs 2026-06-20 (ADR-0005). SOURCING: vanilla layer = output of a recipe in sources.yaml with uniform schema {kind: scaffolder|recipe, ordered steps[], gitignore: <stem>, normalize: [...], resolved: {written by update only}}. Single-scaffolder stacks = one-step recipe. go-api-chi is kind:recipe = pinned checkout of golang-standards/project-layout + go mod init + go get of pinned chi/zap/viper/testify; its wired main.go+handler_test live in the OVERLAY, not vanilla. gitignore mapping: Go->Go.gitignore, Python->Python.gitignore, C#->VisualStudio.gitignore, resolved against ONE repo-wide pinned SHA (top-level gitignore_repo key). PACKAGING (path-rule boundary): everything under templates/golden/<key>/.mkproj-overlay/ is the vetted, refresh-immune layer; everything else under the stack dir is vanilla/refreshable. Overlay files may layer INTO vanilla dirs (init composes vanilla-first then overlay-wins). The walking-skeleton test ships in the overlay next to the wiring it exercises (satisfies non-vacuous-test note + uuw). 3tt owns tree shape + vanilla content + declaring overlay slots; x2k/02o/ud1 own specific overlay files placed within it.
notes: Walking-skeleton model (ADR-0005): shipped artifact must RUN end-to-end with a green test after init; this is the acceptance guarantee on composed vanilla+overlay, not a third layer.

title: Package common scaffold assets and agent settings
description:
## Why this exists
The spec treats common scaffold assets as first-class artifacts: `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md` symlink target content, `.claude/settings.json`, `.claude/settings.local.json.tmpl`, `.codex/hooks.json`, `skill-manifest.json.tmpl`, ADR scaffold, `CONTEXT.md`, and base gitignore content.

## What to package
Create and/or organize the common embedded assets under the mkproj template tree, including initial agent/security settings.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] common scaffold assets are committed in the expected template/common layout
- [ ] `AGENTS.md` and other rendered templates use the approved variable model
- [ ] initial scaffolded security settings follow ADR-0002 plus the allowlist seed (`Bash(tool:*)`, deny-only guard assumptions, personal rules omitted by default)
- [ ] `.codex/hooks.json`, `skill-manifest.json`, ADR scaffold, `CONTEXT.md`, and base gitignore content are included without machine-specific absolute paths
- [ ] this issue owns initial asset packaging only, not later `sync-allowlist` reconciliation
notes: Skill-manifest topology decided 2026-06-19 (grill-with-docs, Q16): OPTION A — SINGLE shared manifest. One committed .claude/skill-manifest.json is the sole lockfile; 'instill check-skills' regenerates BOTH .claude/skills/ AND .agents/skills/ symlink trees from it (confirmed behavior — instill lands only a single manifest file and populates both trees). No per-agent manifest, no drift. Both gitignored (.gitignore lines 7-8 already cover both skills/ trees); manifest is the committed portable lockfile per spec §8.

title: Define mkproj CLI, prompt, and render contract
description:
## Why this exists
The specs define the required inputs and overall interaction model, but the exact command/help/prompt/render contract is still an open implementation item. A junior engineer should not have to infer those rules from prose.

## What to define
Lock the contract for:
- top-level commands (`mkproj`, `mkproj init`, `mkproj update`)
- default command behavior
- prompt order and flag-preanswer rules
- which v1 stacks appear in interactive selection
- canonical render variables and file roles (`render`, `verbatim`, `link`, `delegate`)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `mkproj` defaulting to `init` is explicit
- [ ] `mkproj init` and `mkproj update` behavior/help text is specified
- [ ] prompt flow and flag precedence are explicit for project name, language, type, stack, author identity, and remote
- [ ] only the shipped v1 stacks are exposed in v1 interactive flow; unsupported stacks fail clearly
- [ ] canonical render variables, defaults, and file-role categories are written down so later beads do not invent them
notes: Contract decided 2026-06-19 (grill-with-docs): see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-19-mkproj-cli-prompt-render-contract.md. Variable schema (seed vs derived), command surface, prompt order + three-state precedence, render contract (missingkey=error, .tmpl convention, verbatim security scripts), and all BDD acceptance scenarios are pinned there.

title: Freeze mkproj v1 catalog matrix to Go/Python/C# only
description:
## Why this exists
The approved design ships only Go, Python, and C# in v1. TypeScript, Rust, and Bash remain future scope until their guideline-first expansion work happens later.

## What to do
Encode the shipped v1 stack list and make the boundary executable for both the catalog and the picker.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] the shipped v1 stack list is explicit: `go-cli-cobra`, `go-api-chi`, `python-cli-typer`, `python-fastapi`, `csharp-cli`, `csharp-webapi`
- [ ] TypeScript, Rust, and Bash are clearly deferred and non-selectable in v1 work
- [ ] any future-language expansion is treated as follow-on work rather than implied by the catalog table in the spec

title: Align beads with current specs for junior Go execution
description: Why: the current open beads may have drifted from the latest design specs under docs/superpowers/specs. What: review the specs as source of truth, compare them against current bead descriptions, resolve domain-review conflicts, and rewrite affected beads so a junior Go engineer can execute them without rereading the specs.
acceptance_criteria: All touched beads trace to spec sections, conflicts are resolved explicitly, and updated bead text is junior-engineer-friendly.

title: secret-scan.sh: scan-staged mode + minimal lefthook.yml wiring
description:
## Parent
agentic_template_start-79r (shared secret-scan core).

## Scope
Second vertical slice of the shared secret-scan core. Extend `.claude/hooks/secret-scan.sh` with `scan-staged` and seed the **first** `lefthook.yml` for this repo.

This issue owns only the git-layer caller and the minimal hook file. It MUST NOT add lint/format/test gates; `agentic_template_start-x2k` extends this `lefthook.yml` later. It MUST NOT create `.claude/hooks/guard`.

## What to build
- `scan-staged` mode in `.claude/hooks/secret-scan.sh`
- minimal `lefthook.yml` containing only the secret-scan pre-commit job
- a header comment in `lefthook.yml` stating that `agentic_template_start-x2k` extends this file with lint/format/test gates rather than recreating it

`scan-staged` reads `git diff --cached --name-only` and blocks the commit (`exit 2` + stderr list) if any staged file path matches a secret pattern. Not-a-git-repo / no-staged-files exits 0.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] staging a dotenv file then committing is blocked by the lefthook pre-commit job
- [ ] staging a `.key` file is blocked; staging a dotenv example/template file commits clean
- [ ] staging only `README.md` commits clean; no staged files / outside a git repo exits 0
- [ ] `lefthook.yml` contains only the secret-scan pre-commit job plus the x2k header comment
- [ ] bats coverage for `scan-staged` is green; a real staged-secret lefthook run is verified before close

title: secret-scan.sh: scan-command mode + shared pattern engine + bats harness
description:
## Parent
agentic_template_start-79r (shared secret-scan core).

## Scope
First vertical slice of the shared secret-scan core. Build the source-repo script at `.claude/hooks/secret-scan.sh` with the shared configuration (`SECRET_PATTERNS`, `EXEMPT_PATTERNS`, `ENV_DUMP_VERBS`), the shared matcher (`is_exempt`, `matches_secret`), and the `scan-command` mode.

This issue owns the **source-repo** bats harness only (`mise.toml` with `bats`, plus `test/secret-scan.bats`). It MUST NOT create `lefthook.yml`, MUST NOT create `.claude/hooks/guard`, and MUST NOT own scaffolded template `mise.toml` files for generated repos.

Follow ADR-0002 plus the allowlist/deny-floor seed corrections: use `Bash(tool:*)` syntax elsewhere, keep the guard deny-only, and implement D9/D10 as a path-token scan and env-dump detection rather than hook-side allow logic.

## What to build
- `.claude/hooks/secret-scan.sh`
- shared config arrays and matcher helpers
- `scan-command` reading guard JSON from stdin (`.tool_input.command`) with `--command` fallback
- D9 path-token blocking for secret-bearing paths regardless of binary
- D10 blocking for bare env-dump verbs
- `mise.toml` test harness for this source repo (`bats` tool)
- `test/secret-scan.bats`
- documented irreducible gaps comment block (obfuscated interpreter paths, raw `git cat-file -p <sha>`, etc.)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A guard JSON payload whose command displays a dotenv file exits 2 with a D9 reason
- [ ] `awk` on a dotenv file, `git show` of a dotenv blob, display of a `.pem` file, and a command referencing another shared-pattern secret token such as `.aws/credentials` or `id_ed25519` all block
- [ ] bare `env`, `printenv`, `set`, and at least one extended env-dump verb such as `export -p`, `declare -xp`, or `compgen -v` block; a filtered `env | grep -v SECRET` is allowed
- [ ] dotenv example/template files are allowed; `README.md` is allowed; empty stdin exits 0 without crashing
- [ ] bad subcommand exits 64
- [ ] the source-repo bats suite is green and written red-green-refactor style

title: Implement shared secret-scan core used by guard and lefthook
description:
## Scope
Roll-up issue for the shared secret-scan core that both the future guard hook and lefthook pre-commit consume.

This issue does **not** create `.claude/hooks/guard`. The full deny-only guard hook is tracked separately. This issue closes only when the shared script exists with both caller modes and the shared matcher/config live in one place.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `agentic_template_start-aa9` and `agentic_template_start-02o` are complete
- [ ] `.claude/hooks/secret-scan.sh` exposes both `scan-command` and `scan-staged`
- [ ] the shared config and matcher are defined once and reused by both modes
- [ ] the implementation remains limited to the secret-scan slice of the deny floor (D9/D10 plus shared pattern plumbing), with full guard-hook enforcement tracked separately
- [ ] bats coverage and at least one real pre-commit secret-block verification are complete before close

title: Verify beads git hooks survive lefthook install (chain mode)
description:
## Why this exists
ADR-0003 and the mkproj design require `lefthook install` to run **after** `bd init` in chain/non-clobber mode so beads' existing hooks still execute. This issue proves that behavior before mkproj init wiring depends on it.

## What to verify
Use a fresh fixture repo and verify the exact chain-mode mechanics:
- run `bd init`
- seed a minimal `lefthook.yml` like the one from `agentic_template_start-02o`
- run `lefthook install` using the intended non-clobber/chain mode
- inspect the resulting hook files and exercise a commit/push path that proves both lefthook logic and beads' pre-existing hooks still run

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] the exact hook files checked are named in the issue notes or closeout
- [ ] verification proves whether beads hook files still exist after `lefthook install`
- [ ] verification proves whether both beads behavior and lefthook behavior execute on the same repo after install
- [ ] the exact commands used and the observable pass/fail signals are documented
- [ ] if chain mode fails for any hook type, a follow-up issue is filed before this issue closes

title: Define canonical allowlist + deny-floor seed contents
description: Open item from design §9. Enumerate the embedded canonical allow globs (bd, instill, git read/write, grep/rg/find, mise, gw, slack-cli, rtk — broad prefix per ADR-0002) and the deny floor D1-D10 (incl. secret-exposure D9/D10). This is the 'day one it just works' contents that the reconciler ships.
notes:
Seed contents decided 2026-06-18 (grill-with-docs). Full spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-18-allowlist-deny-floor-seed.md

Key decisions:
- Glob format Bash(cmd:*) colon form; param-form Bash(command:rm *) is silently ignored by Claude Code.
- Compounds auto-approve NATIVELY (matcher is shell-operator-aware) — reverses ADR-0002 premise; seed job is just to make allowlist complete.
- Allowlist = universal dev baseline + per-stack language slice + tagged-optional personal section (stripped unless --include-personal).
- Decided IN: git rm/mv, bash:*, sed/awk. Decided OUT (denied): curl/wget (only exfil control left; use WebFetch).
- Secret protection = whole-command-line path-TOKEN scan (port guardrails-bin), NOT command-name list (research-confirmed bypassable).
- 4 defense layers seeded: (1) native Read/Edit/Write secret matchers, (2) path-token guard hook, (3) OS sandbox BOTH agents (portable: Claude settings.json + Codex config.toml), (4) interpreter-class deny.
- Sandbox: network ON, FS-isolation-only (network-off breaks push/installs); egress controlled by guard D11 exfil deny.
- Deny floor expanded to D1-D16 (added env-dump, exfil, system/process, docker, history, interpreter).
close_reason: Seed contents fully specified in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-18-allowlist-deny-floor-seed.md; ADR-0002 amended for research findings.

title: Add Makefile with build/test/install/uninstall/clean targets
description: mkproj has no Makefile. Add one so 'make install' builds the host binary and places it in $HOME/.local/bin (on PATH). Completions are out of scope. No version/ldflags, no cobra migration. Targets: help (default), build (go build -o bin/mkproj ./cmd/mkproj), test (GOCACHE-pinned go test ./... -count=1), install (build then install -m 0755 to BINDIR ?= $HOME/.local/bin, mkdir -p first), uninstall, clean. All .PHONY. Ensure bin/ is gitignored.
design: BINDIR ?= $(HOME)/.local/bin (overridable escape hatch, fixed default per user). Use install(1) for atomic 0755. install depends on build. Host-platform only, no cross-compile (YAGNI). No completions, no ldflags, no cobra.
acceptance_criteria: make install builds and copies mkproj to $HOME/.local/bin; 'which mkproj' resolves there; make build produces bin/mkproj; make test runs full suite; make clean removes bin/; make uninstall removes installed binary; make help lists targets; bin/ gitignored; go test ./... passes

title: Document mkproj usage and contributor workflow
description:
## Why this exists
The repo now has a full mkproj v1 implementation, but it still lacks a contributor-facing README and the root AGENTS.md is carrying too much mixed-purpose context. New users and agents need a clear, current path for how to use mkproj, how to contribute safely, and where project-specific agent guidance lives.

## What needs to be done
Create a thorough root README.md covering how to use mkproj and how to contribute to this repository. Improve the root AGENTS.md using a WHAT/WHY/HOW structure inspired by the claude-md-architect guidance, keeping it concise and universally applicable while linking to deeper docs where needed.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] root README.md explains what mkproj is, how to run it, how to verify generated output, and how to contribute changes to the repository
- [ ] root AGENTS.md is rewritten or significantly improved using a concise WHAT/WHY/HOW structure
- [ ] AGENTS instructions remain project-specific, universally applicable, and do not duplicate unnecessary global guidance
- [ ] verification commands documented in README and AGENTS match the real repository workflow

title: FEATURE: Maintainer stack refresh is idempotent
description: Integration Feature owning the update-idempotence seam (SPEC.md §18): the steps interpreter (369) produces the vanilla layer that the normalization/refresh path (cjl) must make byte-deterministic. This Feature's gate is the run-twice idempotence test — it can only be proven by running both together (ADR-0006).
acceptance_criteria: SEAM TEST (gate): 'mkproj update --stack <key>' run twice with no upstream change yields templates/golden/<key>/ byte-identical, .mkproj-overlay/ untouched, and an empty git diff. Refresh seam fails loud on orphan, warns on collision. All child stories (369, cjl) closed.

title: Implement mkproj update steps interpreter (run/checkout/strip)
description:
## Why this exists
ADR-0005 commits mkproj update (maintainer path) to executing a recipe's ordered steps[] from sources.yaml. Each row's recipe is a uniform schema with step verbs that need an interpreter. This is maintainer-path surface area distinct from cjl's refresh/determinism orchestration; filed discretely per grill-with-docs 2026-06-20.

## What to build
A small step interpreter invoked by mkproj update that executes, in order, the step verbs declared on a sources.yaml row:
- run: <shell> — invoke a pinned native scaffolder / toolchain command in the temp build dir (e.g. cobra-cli init, go mod init, go get ...)
- checkout: <repo> + ref: <SHA> — clone a pinned git ref into the temp dir
- strip: [globs] — remove paths (.git, README, docs, examples) from the checked-out tree
Steps run in a temp dir; output becomes the vanilla layer (before normalization, ADR-0006). Fails loud naming any missing tool (prompt-render-contract §8).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] run/checkout/strip verbs execute in declared order against a sources.yaml row
- [ ] single-scaffolder stacks (one-step recipe) and multi-step recipes (go-api-chi) both work through the same interpreter
- [ ] missing native scaffolder/tool exits non-zero naming the tool; no snapshot or sources.yaml mutation
- [ ] interpreter runs only on the maintainer update path, never at init
- [ ] output feeds the ADR-0006 normalization pass (interpreter does not normalize)
notes: Step schema per ADR-0005: a sources.yaml row's steps[] use two verbs — 'checkout' (clone a pinned git ref; optional per-step 'strip:' removes paths like .git/README/docs) and 'run' (invoke a pinned scaffolder/toolchain command). 'strip' is a sub-key of a checkout step, NOT a third top-level verb. Single-scaffolder stacks are a one-step recipe through the same interpreter (no shape branching).

title: Publish 6ms guideline updates from ai_support on a safe branch
description: The 6ms slice edited /Users/poconnor/peter_code/ai_support/guidelines/{golang,python,csharp}.md to satisfy overlay traceability. Those edits must be durably published so mkproj's conformance test (ebp) has a stable canonical source to read. The ai_support repo is dirty on main with extensive UNRELATED changes — this issue must isolate only the guideline edits. NOTE (floor semantics, ADR-0010/SPEC §10.2): the guideline is the FLOOR; overlay extras (FluentAssertions, pytest-mock) need NO guideline edit, so this issue's scope is publish-only, not author-new-tools.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN ai_support has unrelated uncommitted changes, WHEN publishing, THEN only the guidelines/{golang,python,csharp}.md edits are staged and unrelated changes remain untouched and uncommitted; AND the guideline edits are committed to a named branch and pushed to the ai_support remote — if no remote exists, that is surfaced as a blocking finding with a filed follow-up issue, never silently skipped; AND mkproj's ebp conformance test can resolve all three guideline files at their canonical path and parse their MUST/SHOULD lines.

title: Add scaffold smoke verification for shipped stacks
description:
## Why this exists
The specs promise that init in an empty folder yields a working repo and that the first commit/push passes the pipeline. Static checks alone are not enough.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] each shipped v1 stack can be scaffolded into a clean directory
- [ ] generated repos contain the expected enforcement files including `mise.toml`, `lefthook.yml`, and CI workflow where applicable
- [ ] observable workflow checks cover `mise install`, `mise run lint`, `mise run test`, and `mise run ci`
- [ ] hook smoke verifies pre-commit and pre-push call the expected gates without duplicating the chain-mode proof owned by `agentic_template_start-485`
acceptance_criteria: each shipped v1 stack scaffolds into a clean directory; generated repos contain the expected enforcement files (mise.toml, lefthook.yml, ci.yml where applicable); LOCAL hermetic smoke asserts 'mise install' + 'mise run lint/test/ci' pass offline with NO network access; hook smoke verifies pre-commit/pre-push call the expected gates without duplicating the 485 chain-mode proof; FULL golden-path smoke (gh, nightly/opt-in) creates its repo under a throwaway namespace (mkproj-smoke-<runid>), and BLOCKING: teardown deletes that repo in a trap/defer so it fires even on mid-test failure, then asserts the repo no longer exists — no leaked artifacts; AND the test's delete-remote is a separate code path from the product's never-delete-remote (ADR-0009), never wired into product code.
notes: 2026-06-23 completion: local six-stack smoke remains green; gated remote smoke passed with MKPROJ_REMOTE_SMOKE=1 after GitHub auth gained delete_repo. Smoke teardown completed successfully and GitHub now shows no remaining mkproj-smoke-* repos under AvogadroSG1. Acceptance is now satisfied end to end.
close_reason: Local six-stack smoke is green, gated remote smoke passed with teardown verified, and no smoke repos remain on GitHub.

title: Implement mkproj update maintainer refresh path
description:
## Why this exists
`mkproj update` is a maintainer-only online path for refreshing vendored assets and snapshots. It must not be folded into normal init behavior.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `mkproj update` refreshes vendored gitignore assets and regenerates/re-pins shipped catalog snapshots
- [ ] the command is explicitly maintainer-only and online/toolchain-dependent
- [ ] normal init does not call the update path
- [ ] refresh output is deterministic enough to rebuild the binary from committed sources
design: Design closed in grill-with-docs 2026-06-20 (ADR-0006). DETERMINISM: update runs a normalization pass over the VANILLA layer only (overlay is hand-authored, deterministic) before writing the snapshot. Normalization rules are per-stack pattern sets in a normalize: block in the sources.yaml row (variance is tool-specific; no universal stripper). Rule classes: neutralize timestamps/dates to sentinel; replace temp-dir abs path with canonical placeholder; neutralize generated GUIDs/random IDs (dotnet); force LF + canonical ordering + trailing-newline; pin tool-version strings to resolved: version not locally-installed. New variance found empirically (run update twice, diff, add rule until clean). CONTRACT = idempotence test: update --stack <key> run twice with no upstream change => templates/golden/<key>/ byte-identical, .mkproj-overlay/ untouched, git diff empty. captured: means last-CHANGED not last-run (no-op update does not rewrite date). REFRESH SEAM: after regenerating vanilla, check committed overlay paths against new vanilla tree. Orphan (overlay parent dir gone) = HARD FAIL, write nothing, name paths. Collision (new vanilla file at overlay path) = LOUD WARN, complete write, overlay-wins still correct. update NEVER auto-mutates overlay.

title: Author scaffolded mise.toml + local gate tasks for shipped v1 stacks
description:
## Scope
Own the scaffolded `mise.toml` assets and local gate contract for the shipped v1 stacks only: `go-cli-cobra`, `go-api-chi`, `python-cli-typer`, `python-fastapi`, `csharp-cli`, and `csharp-webapi`.

This issue extends the minimal `lefthook.yml` created by `agentic_template_start-02o`; it MUST NOT recreate that file. It also does **not** own CI workflow generation, full catalog packaging, or the source-repo `mise.toml` harness added by `agentic_template_start-aa9`.

## What to build
- scaffolded `mise.toml` assets for each shipped v1 stack
- `[tools]` entries for the stack toolchain plus `lefthook`
- `[tasks]` entries for `lint`, `fmt`, `test`, and `ci`
- final local hook mapping: `pre-commit = secret-scan + lint + format`, `pre-push = full tests`
- a clear boundary that the source-repo secret-scan test harness remains owned by `agentic_template_start-aa9`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] only the shipped v1 stacks are in scope
- [ ] each shipped v1 stack has scaffolded `mise.toml` content with `lint`, `fmt`, `test`, and `ci` tasks
- [ ] `lefthook.yml` from `agentic_template_start-02o` is extended rather than recreated
- [ ] task commands trace back to the canonical language guideline files rather than ad hoc defaults
- [ ] the source-repo `mise.toml` used for secret-scan tests remains separate from scaffolded template assets
- [ ] completion is blocked on the chain-mode verification proven by `agentic_template_start-485`
close_reason: Verified on 2026-06-23: shipped v1 overlays carry scaffolded mise.toml/lefthook.yml/ci.yml assets, ci.yml delegates only to 'mise install' then 'mise run ci', and focused verification passed with GOCACHE=/Users/poconnor/peter_code/exploratory_repos/agentic_template_start/.cache/go-build go test ./internal/scaffold ./cmd/mkproj ./test -count=1.

title: Build allowlist reconciler (mkproj sync-allowlist) + dual-agent stale notify
description:
## Scope
Own `mkproj sync-allowlist` plus stale-version detection and notify-only SessionStart wiring for both Claude and Codex.

This issue does **not** own the initial scaffold render of `.claude/settings.local.json` or other common assets. It starts from the scaffolded security settings produced elsewhere and rewrites only the versioned managed block from one embedded canonical source.

Follow ADR-0002 and the allowlist/deny-floor seed: use `Bash(tool:*)` syntax, keep the guard deny-only, and do not implement hook-side allow logic.

## What to build
- embedded canonical allowlist source with universal, stack-specific, optional personal, and deny-floor sections
- `mkproj sync-allowlist` managed-block rewrite behavior
- version staleness detection
- SessionStart notify-only wiring for Claude and Codex
- optional personal-block handling (`--include-personal`) without making personal rules the default

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] the reconciler rewrites only the managed block and preserves surrounding manual config
- [ ] the managed block is versioned and stale detection compares against that version
- [ ] SessionStart on both Claude and Codex can detect stale config and print the remediation command without mutating files
- [ ] up-to-date repos stay quiet; stale repos notify; no auto-mutation occurs
- [ ] personal rules remain opt-in and are omitted by default
- [ ] descriptions and implementation notes explicitly follow ADR-0002 plus the seed corrections

title: Write mkproj design spec doc
description: Capture the approved brainstorming design for the mkproj scaffolding system into docs/superpowers/specs/.

title: Post-v1 language expansion definition
description:
## Why this exists
The shipped mkproj v1 scope is complete for Go, Python, and C#. The remaining language-guideline work for TypeScript, Rust, and Bash is explicitly post-v1 backlog and SHOULD NOT stay attached to the completed v1 delivery epic.

## What needs to be done
Define the post-v1 language expansion stream for mkproj, including scope, sequencing, and acceptance boundaries for future TypeScript, Rust, and Bash support. This epic starts by housing the guideline-foundation task only; broader expansion work MUST be defined separately before implementation begins.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] post-v1 language expansion has a dedicated epic outside the completed v1 delivery epic
- [ ] the initial guideline-foundation task(s) for TypeScript, Rust, and Bash live under this epic
- [ ] the epic explicitly records that broader expansion definition is still pending and is not silently implied by the guideline task alone

title: Write typescript.md / rust.md / bash.md guideline files for post-v1 expansion
description:
## Scope
Backlog-only prerequisite for expansion **after** the shipped v1 catalog (Go/Python/C#) is complete.

This issue writes guideline files for TypeScript, Rust, and Bash so future template work has a written source of truth. It is **not** a dependency for current v1 delivery and MUST NOT add picker entries, golden snapshots, overlays, CI workflows, or any other live catalog changes in the same issue.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] guideline files exist for TypeScript, Rust, and Bash in the expected guidelines location
- [ ] each guideline is written as a prerequisite for later template work, not as live catalog expansion
- [ ] no mkproj picker/catalog/template/CI changes ship under this issue
